Testimonials.description: Discover how professionals like you are transforming their daily practice
Testimonials.cta_text: Do you want to be part of these success stories?

1.name: Maria Lopez
1.text: QuHealthy has completely changed my healthcare experience. I found an excellent specialist in minutes and was able to book a same-day appointment.

2.name: Dr. Carlos Mendoza
2.text: As a professional, the platform has allowed me to better manage my time and connect with patients who truly need my specific services.

3.name: Laura Sanchez
3.text: Since integrating QuHealthy into our center, we have increased bookings by 40% and significantly improved patient satisfaction.

StorePolicies.title: Cancellation Policies
StorePolicies.subtitle: Define the rules for your patients when booking an appointment.

Section.title: Cancellation Policies
Section.description: Protect your time and reduce no-shows.

flexible.name: Flexible
flexible.text: Cancellations or reschedules are free of charge if made at least 24 hours prior to the scheduled appointment. Cancellations with less than 24 hours notice may be subject to a 50% charge of the consultation fee to cover the reserved time.

moderate.name: Moderate
moderate.text: A minimum of 48 hours notice is required to cancel or reschedule without charge. Cancellations made between 24 and 48 hours before the appointment will incur a 50% penalty. Cancellations with less than 24 hours notice or no-shows will be charged at 100%.

strict.name: Strict
strict.text: All appointments require a non-refundable deposit at the time of booking. If you need to reschedule, you must do so 72 hours in advance for your deposit to be transferred to the new date. No-shows will automatically forfeit the deposit.

Section.editor_label: Legal Text Visible to the Patient
Section.editor_placeholder: Write your cancellation, refund, and rescheduling policies here...
Section.editor_hint: This text will be shown to patients before confirming their appointment and in their confirmation emails.
Section.tip_desc: Having a clear cancellation policy visible at the time of booking reduces no-shows by up to 80%. Be sure to be firm but empathetic.

Terms.title: Terms and Conditions of Use
Terms.intro: Welcome to QuHealthy. These Terms and Conditions govern the access and use of the QuHealthy web and mobile platform, legally operated by Marcos Sandoval Ruiz, a natural person with business activity registered under the laws of the United Mexican States.

sections.title: 1. Acceptance and Modification of Terms
sections.content: By accessing, browsing, registering, or using QuHealthy (hereinafter, the 'Platform'), whether as a Patient ('User') or Healthcare Provider ('Professional'), you expressly agree to be legally bound by these Terms and Conditions and our Privacy Notice unconditionally. QuHealthy reserves the strict right to modify, update, or amend these Terms at any time, unilaterally, notifying through the Platform. Continued use after such modifications constitutes your undeniable acceptance.

sections.title: 2. Nature of the Platform and Medical Disclaimer
sections.content: QuHealthy acts EXCLUSIVELY as a technological and commercial intermediary that provides software infrastructure to connect Users with pre-verified Healthcare Professionals. QUHEALTHY DOES NOT PROVIDE MEDICAL, PSYCHIATRIC, DIAGNOSTIC, TREATMENT, OR DISEASE PREVENTION ADVICE. Any content issued on the Platform does not substitute a doctor's advice. Every doctor-patient relationship, diagnoses, and prescriptions issued are the absolute and exclusive legal and clinical responsibility of the Professionals. In case of a medical emergency, call 911 immediately or go to the nearest hospital; our platform is not designed for emergencies.

sections.title: 3. User Profile, Eligibility, and Security
sections.content: To use the Platform, the User must be of legal age (18 years in Mexico) with full legal capacity. You agree to provide reliable, complete, recent, and accurate information. You are exclusively responsible for maintaining the confidentiality of your account and password; any transaction or action under your account will be deemed your own and valid. QuHealthy reserves the right to revoke access to accounts with fraudulent behavior or moral and legal breach to these terms.

sections.title: 4. Stable Obligations of the Healthcare Professional
sections.content: Every Healthcare Professional ('Professional') registered on QuHealthy is legally obliged to comply with current Mexican health regulations imposed by COFEPRIS, the General Health Law, and competent bodies. Professionals declare and guarantee under perjury penalty that they have a valid professional degree, professional license (cedula) registered with the National Registry of Professionals (SEP), and any corresponding permit or certification. QuHealthy executes periodic reviews (KYC) but is not a perpetual guarantor of such credentials; any act or omission derived from the service falls civilly and criminally on the medical expert.

sections.title: 5. Booking, Payments, Cancellations, and Billing
sections.content:
Using the calendar generates an Appointment intention, validated by a fee set independently by the Professional. QuHealthy uses secure third-party payment technology (e.g., Stripe). You agree that the Platform makes the respective transactional charge to your authorized and valid payment method.
General Cancellation Policies:
(a) User cancellations with +24 hrs: Full refund applicable.
(b) User cancellations / 'No-shows' with -24 hrs: The Professional has the right to retain 100% or a percentage defined in their policies, without QuHealthy intervening in monetary mediations beyond the initial credit.
(c) Professional Cancellations: Full refund guaranteed or free rescheduling. Accounting invoices for the provision of medical services are issued directly by the Professional, not by QuHealthy.

sections.title: 6. Telemedicine, Permitted Use, and Prohibited Conduct
7. Intellectual and Industrial Property
sections.content: All inherent rights, trademarks, patents, logos, data compilations, visual interfaces, underlying code (source-code) and algorithms present in QuHealthy belong exclusively to Marcos Sandoval Ruiz. You receive only a limited, non-transferable, and essential license to use the software at the client/user level; reverse engineering, copying, subleasing, data mining, or systematic extractions are impossible.

sections.title: 8. Third-Party Links and Integration Modules
sections.content: Occasionally, QuHealthy links or hosts software and modules provided by third-party companies (laboratories, pharmaceuticals, credit card processors). We disclaim all liability for the content, technical interruption, or security issues of external providers.

sections.title: 9. Systemic Limitation of Liability and Indemnification
sections.content: TO THE MAXIMUM EXTENT PERMITTED BY FEDERAL LAW AND JURISPRUDENCE, IN NO EVENT AND UNDER NO LEGAL THEORY WILL QUHEALTHY AND ITS OPERATORS BE LIABLE TO YOU OR ANY THIRD PARTY FOR DIRECT, INDIRECT, PUNITIVE, INCIDENTAL, EXEMPLARY, OR SPECIAL DAMAGES, INCLUDING MEDICAL NEGLIGENCE, PHYSICAL INJURY, LOSS OF PROFIT, AND DATA LOSS. As a user and healthcare professional, you agree to defend, indemnify, and hold the Platform completely harmless against complaints resulting from the misuse of its portal.

sections.title: 10. Applicable Law and Consolidated Dispute Resolution
sections.content: For the validation, execution, compliance, and material interpretation of these guidelines, the express will to regulate it under the Commerce Code, Federal Civil Code of Mexico, and the General Health Law is stacked, and to resolve contingencies or user lawsuits, the parties designate exclusively the courts of Mexico City, discarding jurisdiction that, due to subsequent or parallel domicile, assigns them. Before initiating legal contingencies, the User agrees to exhaust conciliatory means through customer service portals for a period of no less than 30 days, and otherwise conciliate with competent decentralized bodies such as PROFECO or CONAMED.

Privacy.title: Privacy Notice
Privacy.intro: In strict compliance with the provisions enshrined in the Federal Law on Protection of Personal Data Held by Private Parties (LFPDPPP), its Regulations and derivative guidelines, QuHealthy, a platform operated by Marcos Sandoval Ruiz, extends this Comprehensive Privacy Notice for the benefit and knowledge of our Users and Healthcare Professionals.

sections.title: 1. Identity and Legal Domicile of the Controller
sections.content: Marcos Sandoval Ruiz (operator and natural person with business activity responsible for the 'QuHealthy' brand), with face-to-face operational control headquarters in Mexico City, hereinafter 'The Controller', serves as the official entity in charge of collecting, safeguarding, processing, or revoking your IT, personal, and sensitive biomedical/clinical data.

sections.title: 2. Institutional Regulatory Framework for Data Privacy and Telemedicine
sections.content: For IT transactional purposes of Telemedicine records and Clinical Records, the Platform assimilates the mandatory stipulations raised in the country's regulations, including, without limitation, NOM-024-SSA3-2012 for Electronic Clinical Records (ECE) of the National Health System and the guide on COFEPRIS and Ministry of Health guidelines. All interfaces pursue encrypted regulations comparable in degree to international level directives (e.g., HIPAA guidelines).

sections.title: 3. Typology of Personal and Sensitive Data Subject to Processing
sections.content:
We actively identify, collect, and process various types of your information, whether captured from a direct, indirect, or observed source:
- Physical and Digital Identification Data: First name, pseudonyms, RFC, CURP (as applicable), video call image, and IP device metadata.
- Contact and Billing Data: Residential delivery address, current email, phone number, and standardized tax record.
- SENSITIVE CLINICAL and Patrimonial Data: History of ailments, hereditary and individual pathologies, organic prognosis and information, allergies and formulated prescriptions, as well as numerical codes for fund dispersion or bank cards. We comprehensively understand and guard such dimensions under strict explicit controls due to their intimate nature.

sections.title: 4. Analytical, Clinical, and Commercial Purpose of Processing
sections.content:
The unalienable primary purposes for legal perfection with the Controller encompass:
(a) Validation of profiles and identities (KYC).
(b) The tech and programmatic orchestration face-to-face, remote (video) or asynchronous to allow clinical consultations.
(c) The processing, bank capture, billing, and operational dispatch of management to providers.
The accessory secondary purposes, over which the User is full owner of subsequent refusal without compromising their consultations, include:
Targeted marketing of integrated medical services, novelties notifications (newsletters), Big Data compilation and creation and public health statistics (generalized and NON-nominative algorithmic data-mapping) for market analytical purposes.

sections.title: 5. Restrictions on Transfer to Third Parties and Consent
sections.content:
Your personal data, particularly those classified as sensitive (pathological state), WILL NOT BE COMMERCIALIZED, SOLD OR LICENSED TO THIRD PARTIES alien to the base or operative provision. We will only share data and corporately justified transfers to the following related elements:
(a) Healthcare Providers and Clinics: Specialists explicitly cited or authorized through the proprietary Scheduling software.
(b) Regulated Payment Processors, to process the capture in authorized accounts and with interbank encryption guarantee (e.g., Stripe Inc. or homologous open-source gateway entities).
(c) Legal and Governmental Compliance: By mandate and subjection to Mexican ministries and authorities by firm ministerial mandate, a case on which we abide full revelation.

sections.title: 6. Limitation of Capacity, Use, and Disclosure of Clinical Criteria
sections.content: The Controller maintains exhaustive internal protocols and instills in its chain of workers abstention and corporate secrecy in exploring vital metrics, so that neither its systems nor authorized doctors exploit them for their profit outside your record. You consent upon accepting and scheduling that your authorized specialist keeps the protocol derived from doctor-patient interactions.

sections.title: 7. Inalienable Exercise of 'ARCO' Rights and Revocations
sections.content:
You exercise total prerogative of the material usufruct regarding your collected asset, named by acronym by government precept A.R.C.O. (Complete Access to validate it, Obligatory Rectification in faults or lags, Total or segregated Cancellation of organic flows, or Opposition over dictated secondary optative purposes).
Any derivative procedure requires formal contact through the Official Personal Data Holder, by verified email to: privacy@quhealthy.com accrediting your identity and foundation. Its resolution and probatory issuance takes a maximum term no greater than 20 business days in its course for stipulating resolution.

sections.title: 8. Use of Cookies, Tracking Pixels, and Transactional Intelligence
sections.content: In conjunction with its flow of explicit nominal data, the 'QuHealthy' web operates functional, marketing, and web traffic statistics Cookies. They operate automatically, inserting alphanumeric chains of anonymous collection on your provision server and visiting browser. You can modulate them, reject non-critical cookies in initial panels of the web application's Front-end, or block them proactively from the browser; although disabling core Session cookies will cause your bookings and accounts to not operate, without incurring fault and commitment to the application.

sections.title: 9. Cryptovolumetric and Technical Security Measures
sections.content: The assets and clusters where we host your records and usage variables boast superior architectural safeguards equivalent in lineage, asymmetric perimeter rigor, and cyber-certificate logic to banking entities; records carry cutting-edge double-sided encryption algorithms, ensuring that damage from piracy or external theft does not result in textual exfiltration of the medical report that is deductible to an individual user. We maintain constant cyber resilience audit reports and protocols.

sections.title: 10. Alteration and Issuance of Notification on Temporary Modifiers
sections.content: Given the imperative dynamic and adaptability to legislative scenarios in corporate e-Health law in the federation, the Platform protects and exercises its exclusive right to replacement and perfection in whole or partial sections of this legal body and its Privacy annexes. Every change is visible in the upper date ('Last Update', current header). If required for a substantial express consent of the record management, the portal will notify previous interruptions in modal format or in situ virtual confirmation to the patient upon initiating subsequent web entries.

Cookies.title: Cookie Policy
Cookies.intro: QuHealthy uses cookies and similar technologies to improve navigation and the services of our platform.

sections.title: 1. What are Cookies?
sections.content: Cookies are small text files that are downloaded and stored on the user's device (computer, smartphone, tablet) when accessing certain web pages. They allow the platform to remember information about your visit, such as your preferred language, logged-in session, and other options.

sections.title: 2. Types of Cookies used by QuHealthy
sections.content: a) Strictly Necessary Cookies: Essential for the operation of the platform (e.g., login, load balancer, security). b) Performance and Analytics Cookies: Help us understand how visitors interact with the website, collecting metric and technical information (such as Google Analytics). c) Customization Cookies: Remember user interface settings, such as dark mode or region. d) Advertising Cookies: Allow showing relevant advertising based on your interests (if applicable in the future).

sections.title: 3. Managing and Deleting Cookies
sections.content: You can allow, block, or delete cookies installed on your device by configuring your browser options. Please note that if you disable strictly necessary cookies, some essential functions of the platform (such as bookings) may not operate correctly.
